hooks.command:
cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null || exit 0; timeout 5 git fetch --quiet 2>/dev/null; branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null); upstream=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref @{u} 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; behind=$(git rev-list --count HEAD..@{u} 2>/dev/null || echo 0); [ "$behind" = "0" ] && exit 0; commits=$(git log --pretty=format:"=== %h | %an, %cr%n%s%n%b%n" HEAD..@{u} 2>/dev/null | head -200); stat=$(git diff --stat HEAD..@{u} 2>/dev/null | head -30); jq -n --arg b "$branch" --arg u "$upstream" --arg n "$behind" --arg c "$commits" --arg s "$stat" '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "SessionStart", additionalContext: ("This local checkout is " + $n + " commit(s) BEHIND " + $u + " on branch " + $b + ".

--- New commits (subject + body) ---
" + $c + "
--- Files changed ---
" + $s + "

Greet the user, then SUMMARIZE in plain language what the new commits are about — group related changes and lead with the headline change. Then ask whether they want to run `git pull` to sync. Do NOT run git pull automatically — wait for explicit user approval.")}}'
